goal: Add multilingual support to Mainline website

summary.title: Add multilingual routes to the Mainline website
summary.what: Added static multilingual support for English, Chinese, and Spanish across the Mainline website. The change introduces centralized locale data, reusable Astro page components, localized nav and footer UI, language switching, localized docs/spec/comparison/blog routes, translated repo-memory essay pages, and SEO hreflang/canonical alternates.
summary.why: The website needs to reach English, Chinese, and another broadly useful international audience while preserving static SEO-friendly URLs and avoiding duplicated page logic.
summary.user_goal: Support multiple languages on the website: Chinese plus one more popular language.

decisions.chose: Keep English as the default language, add Chinese at /zh/, and add Spanish at /es/.
decisions.rationale: Chinese was explicitly requested; Spanish is a pragmatic third language for broad international reach.

decisions.chose: Use static localized URL prefixes instead of client-side translation.
decisions.rationale: Static routes give search engines canonical language-specific pages and keep the Astro site deployable as plain static output.

decisions.chose: Move repeated page markup into shared Astro components backed by src/i18n.ts.
decisions.rationale: Shared components avoid maintaining three separate copies of each page while keeping localized copy easy to review.

decisions.chose: Emit canonical URLs plus en, zh-CN, es, and x-default hreflang alternates from BaseLayout.
decisions.rationale: Each localized route should point search engines to equivalent pages and avoid duplicate-content ambiguity.

decisions.chose: Translate the first repo-memory essay into Chinese and Spanish rather than linking localized blog indexes back to the English article.
decisions.rationale: The blog is a major SEO and positioning asset, so language support should cover the primary essay, not only the chrome.

rejected.alternative: Browser-side language switching on a single URL.
rejected.reason: It would be worse for SEO, canonical URLs, and shareable localized pages.

rejected.alternative: Duplicate full page templates per language.
rejected.reason: It would make layout and design changes error-prone across languages.

rejected.alternative: Add automatic locale detection and redirects.
rejected.reason: Explicit language URLs are simpler for launch and avoid surprising users or crawlers.

risks: Chinese and Spanish copy was translated during implementation and should receive native-speaker review before high-traffic launch.
review_notes: Verified with npm run build after the i18n changes; Astro check reported 0 errors and 0 warnings.
The build now emits 21 static pages covering English, Chinese, and Spanish versions of the landing, docs, spec, comparison, blog index, and repo-memory essay routes.

architectural_claims: The site now has explicit static locale prefixes for Chinese and Spanish while English remains at root.
BaseLayout owns localized chrome, language-switch URLs, canonical URLs, and hreflang alternates.
Page content is centralized in src/i18n.ts and rendered through reusable Astro components.
behavioral_changes: Users can switch among English, Chinese, and Spanish from the header.
Localized routes exist for the landing page, docs, spec, blog index, two comparison pages, and the first blog essay.
The sitemap now includes the localized static routes generated by Astro.
migration_notes: Existing English route files now delegate to shared localized page components.
The English blog article frontmatter now provides locale and pagePath so ArticleLayout can generate language alternates.
